is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum DHFR mutations; inclusion criteria required monoinfection with P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports DHFR codons 51, 59, and 108 — all curated Pfdhfr target positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives counts/percentages of mutation patterns among 44 samples (e.g. 108-Asn+51-Ile+59-Arg 33 (75.0%); 108-Asn only 2 (4.5%)); text also states all 44 had at least one mutation.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples from the village of Magoda, Tanga region, North-east Tanzania — sub-national town/village level.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: A single therapeutic study undertaken in 1995 — collection within one year.

african_field_samples.evidence: 44 children enrolled in Magoda, Tanga region, Tanzania; field/clinical isolates. Lab strains (K1, FC27, etc.) used only as controls.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical treatment/molecular study of 44 children with PCR-RFLP genotyping.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Second therapeutic study undertaken in 1995 in the same area among the same population as a 1994 study (Rønn et al. 1996); overlap of population with prior collections is possible.

supplement.evidence: Frequencies for DHFR codons 51, 59, 108 are fully provided in Table 1 within the main text.

assessment.summary: Primary study of 44 P. falciparum-infected children in Magoda village, Tanga region, Tanzania (1995), genotyping DHFR codons 51, 59, 108 by PCR-RFLP. Table 1 provides extractable mutation pattern counts/percentages. All curated criteria met; medium duplicate risk due to same-population overlap with the 1994 Rønn et al. study.
